metadata.method_name: Iterated path-consistency closure (FULL) vs NAIVE single-pass intersection (baseline)
metadata.description: Synthetic de-risking of H3 and H4 on consistent-by-construction QCNs read by an exactly-controlled noisy channel. PRIMARY algebra = convex point algebra (PC complete).

C3.interpretation: Zero-FP certificate CONFIRMED: all-sound => gold in output with probability 1.0; collapse never co-occurs with all-sound. Pre-registered 'slope~1' NOT met because gold is retained MORE robustly than J(E) predicts (point-algebra absorption): contains-gold slope on J(E) = 0.657 (<1), so the certificate is conservative and over-delivers rather than failing.

allen.note: PC is sound-but-incomplete for full Allen; collapse rates are a LOWER BOUND on detectable contradictions. The convex point arm is exact.

overall_verdict.notes: H3: iteration (FULL) beats single-pass NAIVE; structural tie at L=2, gap grows with chain length and cyclomatic number (anchor mechanism: longer/denser networks give iteration more tight-edge anchors NAIVE cannot reach). H4: redundancy net-gain is inverted-U; peak moves outward with recall and under the recall-floor gate; empirical J(E) exceeds the r^E independence model under rho>0, pushing the peak further out. C3: all-sound networks yield gold in Mode-A output with probability 1 (soundness invariant of path-consistency); contains-gold scales ~linearly with J(E).
